title: Behavioral and RT-qPCR Dataset from a Mouse Model of Stress-Induced Dysbiosis and Depression-Like Phenotypes
author: Hassib, Lucas
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This dataset contains the  raw behavioral data  and  quantitative real-time PCR (RT-qPCR) results  from a study investigating the effects of stress-induced gut dysbiosis on behavior and neuroplasticity in mice. The dataset supports findings from a preclinical model of Major Depressive Disorder (MDD), where  fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT)  was used to transfer dysbiotic microbiota from stressed donor mice to healthy recipients. 
  Content Overview:  
 
 
  Behavioral Data : 
 
 
 Novelty Suppressed Feeding (NSF) 
 
 
 Sucrose Spray (SS) 
 
 
 Tail Suspension Test (TST) 
 
 
 Elevated Plus Maze (EPM) 
 
 
 
 
  RT-qPCR Data : 
 
 
 Gene expression levels of Synapsin 1 (Syn1), Oligodendrocyte transcription factor 1 (Olig1), and Sox2 in prefrontal cortex and hippocampus. 
 
 
 
 
  Metadata : includes animal IDs, group assignments (CUS, CTL, CUS-FMT, CTL-FMT), and experimental conditions. 
 
 
  Brief Methodology : 
 Swiss mice were subjected to a  Chronic Unpredictable Stress (CUS)  protocol for 28 days. Fecal samples from CUS and control animals were collected and transplanted into antibiotic-treated recipients for 21 days. Behavioral tests were conducted post-treatment. RT-qPCR was performed to assess gene expression changes in brain regions linked to stress response and neuroplasticity. 
  File Types : 
 
 
 Excel spreadsheets (.xlsx) with raw numerical data.
